archimate:ApplicationService.name: Digital Certification eSignature Service
archimate:ApplicationService.documentation:
Definition: The Digital Certification eSignature Service enables users to securely sign electronic documents using digital certificates. It leverages cryptographic techniques to ensure the integrity, authenticity, and non-repudiation of digitally signed documents. This service provides a user-friendly interface accessible via the web, allowing users to upload their documents, apply their digital signature, and generate a digitally signed version. It utilizes digital certificate authorities to issue and manage digital certificates, providing a trusted framework for electronic signatures. The Digital Certification eSignature Service enhances document security, eliminates the need for physical signatures, and supports the digital transformation of business processes.
Source: ETSI
Source reference: https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_ts/102700_102799/10277801/01.01.01_60/ts_10277801v010101p.pdf
Interoperability Dimension: Structural IoP
LOST view: TVA-Trust Enablers [Motivation]
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DigitalCertificationESignatureServiceApplicationService
ABB name: egovera:DigitalCertificationESignatureServiceApplicationService
EIRA concept: eira:ArchitectureBuildingBlock
Last modification: 2023-11-20
Identifier: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/egovera/DigitalCertificationESignatureServiceApplicationService
Interoperability Layer: TechnicalApplication
Specialises: http://data.europa.eu/dr8/E-SignatureVerificationAndValidationApplicationService

properties.value: The Digital Certification eSignature Service enables users to securely sign electronic documents using digital certificates. It leverages cryptographic techniques to ensure the integrity, authenticity, and non-repudiation of digitally signed documents. This service provides a user-friendly interface accessible via the web, allowing users to upload their documents, apply their digital signature, and generate a digitally signed version. It utilizes digital certificate authorities to issue and manage digital certificates, providing a trusted framework for electronic signatures. The Digital Certification eSignature Service enhances document security, eliminates the need for physical signatures, and supports the digital transformation of business processes.
